fact: 1. **Never inject insulin without carbohydrates immediately available.** Milos tells stories in the [NapsGear AMA](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HupJzVx_-vM) of near-death experiences — chugging 5 C
angle: Insulin & GH protocols — Milos Sarcev framework

fact: 2. **Always have fast-acting sugar on hand.** Glucose tablets, candy, juice, Coca-Cola. This is not optional. Hypoglycemia can cause unconsciousness and death within minutes.

> 📹 **Watch:** [Milos Sarcev — What To Eat Before & After A Workout](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=z4B6r7JIdl8&t=486s) @ **08:06** — The clearest explanation of the nutrient shuttle concept

Milos S
> 📹 **Watch:** [Milos Sarcev — What To Eat Before & After A Workout](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=z4B6r7JIdl8&t=486s) @ **08:06** — The clearest explanation of the nutrient shuttle concept

Milos Sarcev was the first prominent proponent of exogenous insulin use in competitive bodybuilding. His central insight — which he called the "nutrient shuttle" or "hyperemia advantage" — is that ins
The body contains about five to six liters of blood. At rest, roughly 10% of that blood is in the muscles. During an intense bodybuilding-style workout, up to 60-70% of total blood volume accumulates

At [08:06 in the Generation Iron nutrition video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=z4B6r7JIdl8&t=486s), Milos explains this directly: during high-intensity training, a massive amount of blood flows int
This is why Milos does not treat insulin as a post-workout-only tool. His evolved protocol puts the injection *before* the workout, with continuous nutrient intake *during* training.
> 📹 **See also:** [Milos Sarcev — How I Use Insulin for Massive Gains](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jng8U0vZ8LI) — Milos explains his personal protocol on his own channel

> 📹 **See also:** [Milos Sarcev — Insulin Is Highly Effective When Used Properly](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=M8PRd2c7DMs) (Muscular Development) — Milos discusses pioneering insulin as a PED in t

> 📹 **Watch:** [Milos Full Interview — Pancreatic Overload Warning](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CDQHfxYSqXQ&t=2639s) @ **43:59** — Why excessive carbs without insulin control can damage the pancre

> 📹 **Watch:** [Milos Full Interview — Pancreatic Overload Warning](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CDQHfxYSqXQ&t=2639s) @ **43:59** — Why excessive carbs without insulin control can damage the pancre
> 📹 **Watch:** [Milos Full Interview — Insulin Resistance Warning](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CDQHfxYSqXQ&t=2666s) @ **44:26** — How high-carb training routines may strain beta cells
Milos has spoken extensively about combining insulin with growth hormone. From ProfessionalMuscle:

> "GH also acts in a matter that produces hyperglycemia… and coupling these two facts — some of you taking GH and fast acting carbohydrate post workout drinks without exogenous insulin might want to r

At [43:59 in the GI interview](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CDQHfxYSqXQ&t=2639s), Milos warns: excessive carbohydrate consumption can lead to overloading the pancreas, potentially causing insulin r

The logic: GH raises blood glucose (it is diabetogenic). If you're drinking a fast-carb post-workout shake AND using GH, your blood glucose spikes twice — from the carbs and from the GH. Without exoge
Exogenous insulin in this scenario acts as a protective mechanism: it clears the glucose quickly and efficiently, reducing the total time blood sugar is elevated and reducing the strain on endogenous

**GH timing from forum protocols:**
- 2 IU GH with the pre-workout insulin injection
- 2 IU GH before bed

> 📹 **Watch:** [Milos Sarcev Full Interview — Insulin Administration](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CDQHfxYSqXQ&t=2407s) @ **40:07** — Strategic timing of insulin administration

The older method wa
> 📹 **Watch:** [Milos Sarcev Full Interview — Insulin Administration](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CDQHfxYSqXQ&t=2407s) @ **40:07** — Strategic timing of insulin administration
The older method was post-workout injection. Milos moved away from this. His evolved protocol:
1. **60-90 minutes before training:** Eat a small solid meal — ~25g protein, ~25g complex carbohydrates, ~10g fat. This provides a baseline of blood sugar and begins digestion.
2. **Immediately before entering the gym:** Inject insulin. For Humalog, this means the onset hits ~15 minutes into your workout. For Humulin-R IM, onset is similarly fast.

3. **Throughout the entire workout:** Sip the intra-workout shake (the "Magic Drink" — recipe below). You are sipping between every set, finishing by the halfway point of training or continuing throug
4. **Immediately post-workout:** A post-workout shake — 50g whey isolate + 50g carbohydrates (fast-digesting).

5. **30-60 minutes after the post-workout shake:** A solid meal — lean protein (beef, chicken) + complex carbs (sweet potato, rice). ~50g protein, ~50g carbs, trace fats. This meal covers the tail end

Alpha-lipoic acid (ALA) at 300-600mg with carbohydrate-containing meals can improve insulin sensitivity and act as a glucose disposal agent, beneficial during high-carb phases of bodybuilding cycles.
Chromium picolinate at 200-1000mcg/day enhances insulin receptor binding, which can be beneficial in bodybuilding cycles that involve high carbohydrate intake.
Berberine can be used on non-training days to maintain insulin sensitivity, mimicking the effects of metformin.

Milos Sarcev advocates for the use of insulin combined with growth hormone to manage blood glucose levels and enhance nutrient uptake during bodybuilding cycles. Insulin acts as a protective mechanism
Milos Sarcev recommends a specific timing for growth hormone administration: 2 IU with pre-workout insulin and 2 IU before bed.

fact: Milos Sarcev's protocols include the use of LGD-4033 (Ligandrol) as part of a bodybuilding cycle, typically dosed at 10mg daily, to enhance muscle mass and recovery.
Milos Sarcev advocates for the use of insulin combined with growth hormone to manage blood glucose levels and enhance nutrient uptake during bodybuilding cycles.
Alpha-lipoic acid (ALA) at 300-600mg with carbohydrate-containing meals can improve insulin sensitivity and act as a glucose disposal agent, beneficial during high-carb phases of bodybuilding cycles.
Milos Sarcev recommends a specific timing for growth hormone administration: 2 IU with pre-workout insulin and 2 IU before bed.
Humalog insulin has a rapid onset (15 minutes), peaks at 45-75 minutes, and is cleared by 4 hours, making it suitable for peri-workout use.
During intense bodybuilding-style workouts, up to 60-70% of total blood volume accumulates in the working muscle group, which is crucial for nutrient delivery during training.
Chromium picolinate at 200-1000mcg/day enhances insulin receptor binding, which can be beneficial in bodybuilding cycles that involve high carbohydrate intake.
